record.field: Listmonk is a high-performance, self-hosted newsletter and mailing list manager that handles millions of subscribers from a single binary.

Listmonk is a high-performance, self-hosted newsletter and mailing list manager that handles millions of subscribers from a single binary.

Written in Go with a Vue dashboard and backed by PostgreSQL, Listmonk covers the full email-marketing lifecycle: subscriber and list management, campaign creation, and transactional email through a REST API. It targets teams and individuals who want a self-owned, privacy-respecting alternative to hosted services like Mailchimp or Sendy, distinguished by its raw performance, SQL-powered segmentation, and simple single-binary deployment.

Key features
  • Manage millions of subscribers across single and double opt-in lists with custom attributes
  • Segment audiences with powerful, arbitrary SQL query expressions (gated behind a dedicated permission)
  • Create rich HTML and plain-text campaigns with templates, scheduling, and continuous sending
  • Send transactional emails (welcome, receipts, password resets) via a programmable REST API
  • Built-in analytics for opens, clicks, top links, bounce processing, and campaign performance
  • Multi-language UI, granular role and permission controls, and public campaign archive pages
Best for

Businesses, publishers, and developers who need full ownership of their email infrastructure and subscriber data, send newsletters or transactional mail at scale, and prefer a fast, self-hosted platform over third-party SaaS providers.

services: db: image: postgres:17-alpine ports: - 5432 environment: - POSTGRES_USER=listmonk - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=listmonk - POSTGRES_DB=listmonk restart: unless-stopped healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U listmonk interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 6 volumes: - ./listmonk_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal app: restart: unless-stopped image: listmonk/listmonk:v5.0.0 environment: - LISTMONK_app__address=0.0.0.0:9000 - LISTMONK_db__user=listmonk - LISTMONK_db__password=listmonk - LISTMONK_db__database=listmonk - LISTMONK_db__host=db - LISTMONK_db__port=5432 - LISTMONK_db__ssl_mode=disable - LISTMONK_db__max_open=25 - LISTMONK_db__max_idle=25 - LISTMONK_db__max_lifetime=300s - TZ=Etc/UTC depends_on: - db command: - sh - -c - ./listmonk --install --idempotent --yes --config '' && ./listmonk --upgrade --yes --config '' && ./listmonk --config '' volumes: - ../files/config.toml:/listmonk/config.toml - ./listmonk_uploads:/listmonk/uploads container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:9000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0

[app] address = "0.0.0.0:9000"
[db] host = "db" port = 5432 user = "listmonk" password = "listmonk" database = "listmonk"
ssl_mode = "disable" max_open = 25 max_idle = 25 max_lifetime = "300s"
params = ""
